behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on semantic quality of real LLM output (specificity vs genericity), which can vary across runs even with the same prompt/thread. Mocked or stubbed model outputs would hide this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real drafting model on a representative real thread set; assert each draft contains grounded references to thread-specific entities/events (via rubric or LLM-as-judge with human-calibrated checks) and fails when responses are generic.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is between claim extraction/checker logic and KB retrieval/lookup. Real defects are often in module wiring, schema mapping, or contradiction matching, not just one pure function.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local KB fixture plus draft samples containing known contradictions; execute the real checker stack (retrieval + matching + flagging) and assert contradictory claims are flagged with correct references.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Even with correct no-hit retrieval wiring, the key risk is model hallucination under sparse context. Deterministic mocks would miss whether the real model actually follows the disclaimer instruction.
behaviors.test_description: Run generation with real LLM using threads where KB retrieval intentionally returns no relevant facts; assert drafts include the required disclaimer and do not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module gating behavior (UI/action state, orchestration, send service invocation). A unit test of a single guard function is insufficient to catch wiring mistakes that trigger premature send.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the approval flow with real orchestration modules and a test send adapter; verify no send call occurs before explicit approval event, and exactly one send occurs after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full agent pipeline validation across multiple stages, including at least one real agentic generation step plus downstream checks and approval gating.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real email thread source through the full pipeline (ingest, LLM draft, fact-check, approval UI/state); approve and confirm final send artifact matches the approved draft and logged checks.
